description:
You are a data-analyst who must automate the creation of a daily revenue report from several CSV
    source files and make sure the job runs automatically every night by means of cron.

    Starting situation
    ------------------
    1. Three raw CSV files are already present under
       /home/user/data/raw_sales/  
       • sales_2023-03-01.csv  
       • sales_2023-03-02.csv  
       • sales_2023-03-03.csv  
       The exact contents of these files are shown later in this description so that you know the
       input you have to process.  
    2. The directories /home/user/data/summaries/, /home/user/scripts/ and
       /home/user/logs/ exist and are writable by the normal user.

    Goal
    ----
    a) Write an executable shell script called
       /home/user/scripts/generate_daily_revenue_report.sh.  
       • The script **must not** expect any command-line arguments.  
       • It must read every “*.csv” file in /home/user/data/raw_sales/, compute the daily
         revenue for each day (units × unit_price) and then compute the grand total.  
       • It must produce the single output file
         /home/user/data/summaries/daily_revenue_report.txt in the *exact* format shown below
         (pay close attention to the header line, the order of the dates and the fact that all
         monetary values use two decimal places):  

            Date,Daily Revenue
            2023-03-01,455.00
            2023-03-02,384.00
            2023-03-03,400.00
            TOTAL,1239.00

       • If the script completes successfully it must print the line  
         “Report generated successfully” (without the quotes) to standard output.
    b) Make /home/user/scripts/generate_daily_revenue_report.sh executable.
    c) Install a cron entry for the *current user* that runs the script every day at **02:15**
       (local time).  Redirect both stdout and stderr to
       /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log, **appending** to the file.  The resulting crontab entry
       must therefore include “>> /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log 2>&1”.
    d) Run the script manually **once** so that both
       /home/user/data/summaries/daily_revenue_report.txt and
       /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log already exist by the time the automated tests inspect
       the system.

    Exact input data
    ----------------
    /home/user/data/raw_sales/sales_2023-03-01.csv  
        date,product,units,unit_price
        2023-03-01,Widget,10,25.50
        2023-03-01,Gadget,5,40.00

    /home/user/data/raw_sales/sales_2023-03-02.csv  
        date,product,units,unit_price
        2023-03-02,Widget,8,25.50
        2023-03-02,Thingamajig,3,60.00

    /home/user/data/raw_sales/sales_2023-03-03.csv  
        date,product,units,unit_price
        2023-03-03,Gadget,7,40.00
        2023-03-03,Thingamajig,2,60.00

    Verification criteria
    ---------------------
    The automated grader will confirm all of the following:

    1. The file /home/user/scripts/generate_daily_revenue_report.sh exists, is executable and
       begins with a valid shebang (e.g. “#!/usr/bin/env bash”).
    2. Running the script with no arguments produces exit status 0 and the single line  
       “Report generated successfully” on standard output.
    3. /home/user/data/summaries/daily_revenue_report.txt exists and its content *exactly*
       matches the four-line CSV block shown above (no extra blank lines, no spaces).
    4. /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log exists and contains at least one line whose text
       includes the phrase “Report generated successfully”.
    5. `crontab -l` for the current user contains **one** line matching the pattern  
       `15 2 * * * /home/user/scripts/generate_daily_revenue_report.sh`
       and that same line performs “>> /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log 2>&1” redirection.

    Important
    ---------
    • Do **not** hard-code absolute path names inside your script other than the three canonical
      directories and filenames given in this task.  
    • Assume only POSIX shell tools (awk, grep, printf, etc.) are available—no Python, Perl, or
      external CSV libraries.  
    • Do not modify, rename, or move the input CSV files supplied to you.  
    • Ensure your script gracefully handles being run multiple times (it should overwrite the
      previous version of the report file but append to the log file).
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory structure -->
    Directory /home/user/data/raw_sales/ already exists.
    Directory /home/user/data/summaries/ already exists.
    Directory /home/user/scripts/ already exists.
    Directory /home/user/logs/ already exists.

    <!-- Pre-existing raw CSV files and their exact contents -->
    File: /home/user/data/raw_sales/sales_2023-03-01.csv
    ----
    date,product,units,unit_price
    2023-03-01,Widget,10,25.50
    2023-03-01,Gadget,5,40.00

    File: /home/user/data/raw_sales/sales_2023-03-02.csv
    ----
    date,product,units,unit_price
    2023-03-02,Widget,8,25.50
    2023-03-02,Thingamajig,3,60.00

    File: /home/user/data/raw_sales/sales_2023-03-03.csv
    ----
    date,product,units,unit_price
    2023-03-03,Gadget,7,40.00
    2023-03-03,Thingamajig,2,60.00

    <!-- Report file EXPECTED after the agent runs the script once -->
    File: /home/user/data/summaries/daily_revenue_report.txt
    ----
    Date,Daily Revenue
    2023-03-01,455.00
    2023-03-02,384.00
    2023-03-03,400.00
    TOTAL,1239.00

    <!-- Log file EXPECTED to exist; exact timestamp not checked -->
    File: /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log
    It must contain at least one line that includes the literal substring:
    Report generated successfully

    <!-- Cron entry that the grader will look for -->
    The user crontab must include exactly this substring:
    15 2 * * * /home/user/scripts/generate_daily_revenue_report.sh >> /home/user/logs/revenue_cron.log 2>&1

    <!-- Script file checks -->
    File: /home/user/scripts/generate_daily_revenue_report.sh
    • Must have execute permission.
    • Must start with "#!" (a shebang).
